qa_pairs.question: What information is required to recover an account if a user has lost all their multifactor authentication devices and has no bypass codes?
qa_pairs.answer: If a user has lost all their registered multifactor authentication devices and does not have bypass codes, they must email support@tech.alliancecan.ca with answers to several questions. This includes their primary email address, how long they've had an active account, their research area, IP address, sponsoring principal investigator, group members, a contact for validation, the clusters they use most, frequently loaded software modules, the date of their last job on the clusters, recent batch job IDs, and recent technical support ticket topics and IDs.

qa_pairs.question: Which SSH clients can be used with multifactor authentication?
qa_pairs.answer: Most command-line SSH clients, such as those on Linux and macOS, are compatible with multifactor authentication. Specific clients that also support it include Cyberduck, FileZilla, JuiceSSH (on Android), MobaXTerm, PuTTY, PyCharm, Termius (on iOS), VSCode, and WinSCP.
